epic.name: Observability & Health
epic.objective: Implement health checks, metrics endpoint, and structured logging for operational visibility
epic.definition_of_done: Health and readiness endpoints functional, Prometheus metrics exposed, structured JSON logging throughout application

tasks.name: Implement GET /health endpoint
tasks.description: Liveness check that returns OK if server is running

tasks.name: Implement GET /ready endpoint
tasks.description: Readiness check that verifies database and Redis connectivity
acceptance_criteria: Checks PostgreSQL connection with simple query
Returns 503 with failing component if unhealthy

tasks.name: Implement GET /metrics endpoint
acceptance_criteria: Exposes HTTP request metrics (method, path, status, duration)
Exposes business metrics (incidents_total, alerts_total by status/severity)

tasks.name: Implement structured JSON logging
acceptance_criteria: Standard fields: timestamp, level, message, service
Request logs include: method, path, status, duration_ms, request_id
Error logs include: error message, stack trace
Log level configurable via LOG_LEVEL env var

tasks.name: Add request ID middleware
tasks.description: Generate and propagate request IDs for tracing
acceptance_criteria: Generate UUID request ID for each request
Include request ID in all log entries
Pass request ID to downstream services (Slack, etc.)
